name: Trackright
highlight_text: Best for team collaboration and automated updates
description:
What Trackright Does

TrackRight is a local SEO platform that allows highly customizable rank tracking across defined geographic areas and proximity radii. The key differentiator for them is the ability to create custom geo-grids of any shape and size, pinpointing specific neighborhoods or locations to track and optimize local visibility.

‍

Source: Trackright

Key Features

  • Design localized tracking grids in any size/shape
  • Manually place location pins in designated areas and remove irrelevant locations from the geo-grid
  • Leverage pre-built grid templates as starting points
  • Track rankings on Google Maps/Local Pack results and monitor organic local search rankings in parallel
  • Track rankings of all competitors in the same areas and identify specific pages/listings outranking you locally
  • Local ranking position, average rank, share of voice
  • Visibility scoring to identify high-potential areas
  • Average total rank positions across setup
  • Automated reports on customizable cadences and visualizations mapping local ranking dynamics
  • Expert guidance to improve local/GMB optimizations and customized tips for outranking local competitors 

Benefits of TrackRight

  • Pinpoint local tracking to high-impact neighborhoods and make targetted improvements in your listings
  • Unified view of Maps/web local search performance to compare your visibility across different properties  
  • Identify localized areas of opportunity and threats
  • Benchmark against competitors making local inroads
  • Automated reporting tailored to your needs
  • Optimization recommendations based on the ranking data

Pricing

$99 per month

Pros

✅Monitor local competition across the same areas

✅Expert recommendations to drive optimization steps

Cons

❌Too expensive for rank tracking capabilities, may not be suitable for small businesses

‍
summing_up_description:
Summing Up…

A good rank tracking tool gives you all-round visibility and location-specific insights. While added features are a great-to-have, you should also priotize robust infrastructure and support that you can rely on. Instead of switching between multiple tools, it’s best to have a software that stays on top of your local visibility data while also helping you optimize your profiles to rank better.

Get started with Synup

Synup helps you take efficiently manage as well as optimize your local listings with access to high-quality data into your local visibility. Get hyper-targeted data on your local rankings and optimize your business to be present where your customers are.
‍


Best Local Rank Tracking Tools: FAQs

‍

  1. What is a local rank tracker?

A local rank tracker is a tool that allows you to monitor how your business website or listing ranks in Google's local search results across different geographic areas like cities, neighborhoods, zip codes etc. It shows you where exactly you appear in the local map pack, local finder listings, and regular web results when people search for businesses like yours near a certain location. It's super helpful for local businesses to track their visibility and make sure they show up prominently in their service areas.

‍

  1. How do I track local SEO?

To effectively track your local SEO performance, you'll want to use a dedicated local rank tracking tool. These allow you to input your business details and the cities/zips you want to monitor. From there, you can see daily or weekly ranking updates for your tracked keywords across those different geographic areas. Many tools will map out your rankings visually and compare you to competitors. Tracking things like your position in the local pack, your overall visibility across an area, and how that changes over time is key.

‍

  1. How do you rank locally?

Ranking well locally takes a bit of work but starts with nailing the basics – having a complete and optimized Google Business Profile, getting listed on major citation sources like Yelp, Apple Maps etc., and acquiring positive reviews. But technical local factors like having NAP consistency, localized content/landing pages and a fast-loading site built with local intent in mind are huge too. Naturally getting listed in more local/map pack results depends heavily on proximity and relevance to any given search. So you need to make sure your local targeting aligns with your service areas through things like schema markup and localized link building.

‍
